name: Lockheed Martin
description:
Lockheed Martin Corporation is a leading global aerospace and defense company and the largest defense contractor in the world by revenue. The company designs, develops, manufactures, and sustains advanced military aircraft, missile and fire control systems, integrated air and missile defense, rotary and mission systems, and space platforms for the U.S. Department of Defense, U.S. government agencies, and allied nations. Its portfolio spans combat aircraft, helicopters, hypersonics, missile defense, radar and sensor systems, C4ISR, and strategic and commercial space systems.

Formed in 1995 through the merger of Lockheed Corporation and Martin Marietta, the company traces its roots back to early 20th century aviation pioneers. Lockheed Martin plays a central role in flagship U.S. defense programs such as the F-35 Lightning II, the world’s largest fighter aircraft program, and key missile defense architectures like Aegis and THAAD. The company is also a major prime contractor in national security space, supporting missions for NASA, the U.S. Space Force, and intelligence agencies with satellites, space vehicles, and deep-space exploration systems. With a heavy emphasis on research, advanced engineering, and long-term partnerships with government customers, Lockheed Martin is a core pillar of U.S. and allied defense industrial capability.
keyProducts: F-35 Lightning II Joint Strike Fighter
F-22 Raptor air superiority fighter
C-130J Super Hercules airlifter
THAAD (Terminal High Altitude Area Defense)
Aegis Combat System and Aegis Ballistic Missile Defense
notableContracts: F-35 Joint Strike Fighter System Development, Production, and Sustainment for the U.S. and partner nations
Aegis Ballistic Missile Defense and Aegis Combat System modernization for the U.S. Navy and international customers
THAAD missile defense system production and support contracts for the U.S. Missile Defense Agency and allied nations

name: RTX
description:
RTX Corporation, formerly known as Raytheon Technologies, is one of the world’s largest aerospace and defense companies, providing advanced systems and services for military, government, and commercial customers. Formed in 2020 through the merger of Raytheon Company and United Technologies Corporation’s aerospace businesses (Collins Aerospace and Pratt & Whitney), RTX integrates capabilities in missiles and air and missile defense, avionics, communications, engines, sensors, and space systems. The company serves the U.S. Department of Defense, allied militaries, intelligence agencies, and prime contractors worldwide.

RTX plays a central role in U.S. and allied defense modernization, supplying key systems for integrated air and missile defense, precision strike, secure communications, electronic warfare, and space-based capabilities. Its portfolio includes Patriot and NASAMS air defense systems, AMRAAM and Tomahawk missiles, advanced radar and EO/IR sensors, as well as propulsion systems and mission systems for fighters, transports, rotary-wing aircraft, and unmanned platforms. Through its three core businesses—Raytheon, Pratt & Whitney, and Collins Aerospace—RTX underpins critical deterrence, C2, and survivability architectures across air, land, sea, cyber, and space domains.
keyProducts: Patriot air and missile defense system
AMRAAM (Advanced Medium-Range Air-to-Air Missile)
Tomahawk cruise missile
NASAMS (National Advanced Surface-to-Air Missile System)
AN/TPY-2 and SPY-series air and missile defense radars
notableContracts: U.S. and international Patriot air and missile defense production and modernization contracts
U.S. Navy Tomahawk cruise missile production and recertification contracts
AMRAAM production and sustainment contracts for the U.S. and allied air forces

name: Northrop Grumman
description:
Northrop Grumman Corporation is a leading global aerospace and defense technology company that provides systems, products, and solutions in autonomous systems, cyber, C4ISR, strike, and logistics and modernization. The company is a major prime contractor to the U.S. Department of Defense and allied governments, with core strengths in advanced aircraft, strategic deterrence systems, integrated air and missile defense, space systems, and battle management and command-and-control solutions. Its portfolio spans manned and unmanned aircraft, strategic missile and space platforms, advanced sensors and radars, and secure communications and networking.

The company traces its heritage to the early 20th century through predecessor firms such as Grumman Aircraft Engineering Corporation and Northrop Corporation, and took its current form in 1994 with the merger of Northrop and Grumman. Over the decades it has played a central role in U.S. military aviation and space programs, from carrier-based aircraft and the Apollo lunar module heritage to today’s B-2 Spirit and B-21 Raider stealth bombers, the Ground Based Strategic Deterrent/Sentinel ICBM program, and critical national security space missions. As one of the largest U.S. defense contractors, Northrop Grumman is a key contributor to strategic deterrence, missile defense, and space resilience for the United States and its allies.
keyProducts: B-21 Raider long-range stealth bomber
B-2 Spirit stealth bomber
RQ-4 Global Hawk and related high-altitude ISR UAVs
Ground Based Strategic Deterrent (LGM-35A Sentinel) ICBM program
AN/APG-series AESA radars and advanced sensor suites
notableContracts: U.S. Air Force B-21 Raider Long Range Strike Bomber development and production contract
U.S. Air Force Ground Based Strategic Deterrent (LGM-35A Sentinel) engineering, manufacturing, and deployment program
National Security Space missions for the U.S. Space Force and Intelligence Community, including missile warning and protected communications payloads

name: General Dynamics
description:
General Dynamics is a major American aerospace and defense corporation that provides a broad portfolio of products and services to military, government, and commercial customers worldwide. Organized into four primary business groups—Aerospace (Gulfstream), Marine Systems, Combat Systems, and Technologies—the company designs and manufactures business jets, nuclear-powered submarines, armored vehicles, command-and-control systems, and advanced IT and cyber solutions. Its platforms and systems support U.S. and allied forces across land, sea, air, cyber, and space-related domains.

Founded in 1952 and significantly expanded through acquisitions such as Electric Boat, Bath Iron Works, and Gulfstream Aerospace, General Dynamics has become one of the largest defense contractors in the world. The company plays a central role in U.S. naval shipbuilding, armored combat vehicle production, and secure communications networks, supporting programs like the Virginia- and Columbia-class submarines, Abrams main battle tanks, Stryker vehicles, and Gulfstream special mission aircraft. With a strong defense revenue base and a global footprint, General Dynamics is a key supplier to the U.S. Department of Defense, allied militaries, and government agencies, with growing activity in cyber, cloud, and mission IT services.
keyProducts: Gulfstream business and special mission aircraft (e.g., G500/G600/G700)
M1A2 Abrams main battle tank
Stryker 8x8 wheeled combat vehicle family
Virginia-class and Columbia-class ballistic-missile and attack submarines (via Electric Boat)
Arleigh Burke-class destroyer and other surface combatants (via Bath Iron Works)
notableContracts: U.S. Navy contracts for design and construction of Virginia-class and Columbia-class submarines
U.S. Army production and sustainment contracts for the M1 Abrams and Stryker combat vehicle fleets
Multi-billion-dollar IT, cloud, and mission support contracts for U.S. DoD and federal agencies through General Dynamics Information Technology (GDIT)

name: The Boeing Company
description:
The Boeing Company is one of the world’s largest aerospace and defense manufacturers, providing military aircraft, rotorcraft, missiles, satellites, and related systems and services to governments and commercial customers worldwide. Through its Defense, Space & Security (BDS) business, Boeing delivers platforms such as fighter and attack aircraft, tankers, maritime patrol aircraft, rotorcraft, and a wide range of command, control, communications, and intelligence solutions. The company also supports its products with global sustainment, training, and logistics services.

Founded in the early 20th century as an aircraft manufacturer, Boeing has grown into a core pillar of U.S. and allied defense industrial capacity. Its defense portfolio includes flagship programs for the U.S. Department of Defense and international partners, spanning air dominance, strategic airlift, aerial refueling, missile defense, and space systems. Boeing plays a central role in integrating advanced avionics, networked battle management, and space-based capabilities, supporting national security, deterrence, and coalition operations across air, maritime, and space domains.
keyProducts: F/A-18E/F Super Hornet strike fighter
P-8A Poseidon maritime patrol aircraft
KC-46A Pegasus aerial refueling tanker
AH-64E Apache attack helicopter
T-7A Red Hawk advanced trainer aircraft
notableContracts: U.S. Navy F/A-18E/F Super Hornet and EA-18G Growler production and modernization programs
U.S. Air Force KC-46A Pegasus tanker production and lifecycle support contracts
U.S. Navy P-8A Poseidon maritime patrol aircraft production for U.S. and allied fleets

name: L3Harris Technologies
description:
L3Harris Technologies is a major American defense and aerospace company specializing in communications systems, electronic warfare, C4ISR, and integrated mission solutions for air, land, sea, space, and cyber domains. Formed in 2019 through the merger of Harris Corporation and L3 Technologies, the company leverages decades of heritage in tactical radios, sensors, avionics, and surveillance systems to support U.S. and allied warfighters. Its portfolio spans secure tactical communications, electronic warfare and ISR payloads, night vision and soldier systems, avionics and missionization for military aircraft, as well as space payloads and ground systems.

The company plays a critical role in enabling joint all-domain command and control, resilient communications, and advanced sensing for the U.S. Department of Defense, intelligence community, and international partners. L3Harris is a key supplier of tactical radios to the U.S. Army and Special Operations Forces, provides electronic warfare and ISR systems for airborne platforms, and delivers space-based sensors and payloads for national security missions. Through ongoing investments in open systems architectures, software-defined capabilities, and AI-enabled sensor fusion, L3Harris focuses on rapidly fielding interoperable solutions to meet evolving threat environments and modernization priorities worldwide.
keyProducts: AN/PRC-117G and AN/PRC-158 multiband tactical radios
Falcon IV and Falcon III family of tactical communications systems
Viper Shield electronic warfare system for fighter aircraft
WESCAM MX-series electro-optical/infrared (EO/IR) sensor turrets
Classified and unclassified space payloads and ground systems for national security missions
notableContracts: U.S. Army Handheld, Manpack & Small Form Fit (HMS) tactical radio programs, including Leader Radio contracts
U.S. Special Operations Command and multiple allied nation contracts for WESCAM MX-series ISR turrets and mission systems
National security space payload and mission solutions contracts with the U.S. Space Force and intelligence community agencies

name: Leidos
description:
Leidos is a major U.S. defense, technology, and engineering company that provides mission-critical solutions to the Department of Defense, intelligence community, civil agencies, and allied governments. The company’s core capabilities span defense IT and networking, cyber operations, C4ISR, systems integration, digital modernization, and mission software, with a strong focus on complex, highly regulated environments. Leidos supports customers across air, land, sea, space, and cyber domains, integrating advanced analytics, AI/ML, and secure cloud architectures into large-scale enterprise and mission systems.

Formed in 2013 through the split of SAIC (Science Applications International Corporation), Leidos traces its roots back to 1969 and decades of classified and unclassified R&D and systems engineering work for U.S. government agencies. Today it plays a central role in U.S. defense and intelligence modernization, supporting programs such as the Federal Aviation Administration’s air traffic control infrastructure, Department of Defense enterprise networks, and U.S. intelligence community analytic environments. Leidos has also expanded into logistics, autonomous systems, and electronic warfare, positioning itself as a key integrator for multi-domain operations and digital transformation across the defense sector.
keyProducts: Defense & Intelligence IT and Enterprise Network Modernization (including DISA and DoD network programs)
Airborne Reconnaissance and ISR Mission Systems Integration
Leidos Flight Service and FAA NextGen Air Traffic Management Support
Cyber Operations and Defensive Cyber Solutions for DoD and Intelligence Community
Autonomous and Uncrewed Systems Solutions (including maritime and airborne platforms integration)
notableContracts: U.S. Department of Defense Information Network and Enterprise IT Modernization Contracts (including DISA and service-specific network programs)
Federal Aviation Administration NextGen and National Airspace System Support Contracts (including the En Route Automation Modernization follow-on work)
Intelligence Community Mission IT, Analytics, and Multi-INT Processing Support Contracts (various classified programs)

name: HII
description:
HII (formerly Huntington Ingalls Industries) is a major U.S. defense contractor and the largest military shipbuilding company in the United States. Spun off from Northrop Grumman in 2011, HII designs, builds, and maintains nuclear-powered aircraft carriers and submarines, amphibious assault ships, destroyers, and a range of other complex naval platforms, primarily for the U.S. Navy and Coast Guard. The company operates through three main segments: Newport News Shipbuilding, Ingalls Shipbuilding, and Mission Technologies, covering everything from hull construction and lifecycle sustainment to advanced C5ISR, cyber, and unmanned systems.

With a legacy tracing back more than a century through its historic shipyards in Virginia and Mississippi, HII plays a central role in U.S. naval force structure and readiness. Beyond large-deck ships and submarines, HII provides integrated logistics support, training and simulation, defense IT and cyber solutions, as well as autonomous undersea and surface systems. This combination of heavy-industrial shipbuilding and high-end digital and mission technologies positions HII as a critical partner to the Department of Defense, particularly the U.S. Navy, across the full lifecycle of major maritime platforms and associated mission systems.
keyProducts: Ford-class aircraft carriers (e.g., USS Gerald R. Ford CVN-78)
Nimitz-class aircraft carrier construction and refueling & complex overhauls (RCOH)
Virginia-class and Columbia-class nuclear-powered submarines (Newport News Shipbuilding workshare)
Arleigh Burke-class (DDG 51) destroyers and America/San Antonio-class amphibious ships (Ingalls Shipbuilding)
LDUUV and other unmanned maritime systems via Mission Technologies
notableContracts: U.S. Navy contracts for design and construction of Ford-class aircraft carriers, including follow-on carriers CVN-79 and CVN-80
Block procurement and lifecycle support contracts for Virginia-class and Columbia-class ballistic missile submarines in partnership with General Dynamics Electric Boat
Multi-ship U.S. Navy contracts for Arleigh Burke-class destroyers, amphibious assault ships, and long-term maintenance, modernization, and sustainment services across the surface and carrier fleet

name: Amentum
description:
Amentum is a leading U.S.-based government services and engineering contractor that provides mission support, sustainment, and technical services to the U.S. Department of Defense, Department of Energy, Department of Homeland Security, intelligence community, and allied governments worldwide. Formed through the spinout of AECOM’s Management Services business in 2020 and expanded via acquisitions such as DynCorp International and PAE, Amentum has become one of the largest providers of operations, maintenance, logistics, and specialized technical services across land, air, sea, cyber, and nuclear domains.

The company’s work spans base operations support, depot- and intermediate-level maintenance, readiness and training, nuclear and environmental remediation, engineering and modernization, and mission support for complex national security programs. Amentum plays a significant role in sustaining military platforms and infrastructure, supporting contingency and expeditionary operations, and operating highly regulated facilities for the Department of Energy and National Nuclear Security Administration. Through its global footprint and large deployed workforce, Amentum is a key enabler of U.S. and allied defense readiness, lifecycle support, and critical infrastructure resilience.
keyProducts: Global base operations and installation support services for U.S. and allied forces
Aircraft and rotary-wing maintenance, repair, and overhaul (MRO) for DoD fleets
Nuclear facility operations, maintenance, and environmental remediation for DOE/NNSA sites
Contingency, expeditionary, and logistics support for overseas military operations
Cyber, IT, and mission-support services for federal defense and intelligence customers
notableContracts: U.S. Army Logistics Civil Augmentation Program (LOGCAP) support task orders
Operations and mission support contracts at Department of Energy and National Nuclear Security Administration sites (including Savannah River and Hanford-related work through joint ventures)
U.S. Air Force and U.S. Navy aircraft maintenance and flight line support contracts at multiple CONUS and OCONUS bases

name: Booz Allen Hamilton
description:
Booz Allen Hamilton is a leading management and technology consulting firm that plays a major role in U.S. and allied defense, intelligence, and national security missions. The company provides a blend of strategic consulting, digital transformation, cybersecurity, analytics, engineering, and mission operations support to the Department of Defense (DoD), intelligence community, and civilian agencies. Its work spans areas such as cyber defense, AI and machine learning, cloud migration, C4ISR support, modeling and simulation, and enterprise IT modernization.

Founded in the early 20th century as a traditional management consultancy, Booz Allen has evolved into a hybrid consulting and technology integrator with deep domain expertise in defense and intelligence. The firm is particularly known for its classified work supporting cyber operations, signals and geospatial intelligence, and specialized mission systems engineering. Through its combination of consulting heritage and technical capabilities, Booz Allen often acts as a bridge between cutting-edge commercial technologies and the highly secure, mission-critical environments of the U.S. military and intelligence agencies.
keyProducts: Cybersecurity and cyber operations support services for DoD and intelligence community
AI and machine learning solutions under the Booz Allen AI/ML and analytics portfolio
Enterprise IT modernization and cloud migration services for defense agencies
C4ISR and mission systems engineering and integration support
Modeling, simulation, and wargaming/mission analytics services
notableContracts: Multi-year cybersecurity and cyber operations support contracts with U.S. Cyber Command and service cyber components
Intelligence analysis, data analytics, and IT services contracts with the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) and other intelligence community customers
Enterprise IT, analytics, and digital engineering support contracts with the U.S. Navy, Air Force, and other DoD components

name: CACI International Inc
description:
CACI International Inc is a U.S.-based technology and professional services company that provides mission-focused solutions to defense, intelligence, and federal civilian customers. The company is best known for its work in signals intelligence, electronic warfare, cyber operations, secure communications, and enterprise IT modernization. CACI delivers specialized software, advanced ISR and C4ISR capabilities, and data analytics solutions that support U.S. national security objectives, including battlefield decision advantage, multi-domain operations, and information dominance.

Founded in 1962 as California Analysis Center, Inc., CACI has grown through organic expansion and numerous acquisitions into a major mid-tier defense contractor with a strong presence across the Department of Defense, the Intelligence Community, and other federal agencies. The company plays a key role in areas such as counterterrorism, space and airborne ISR processing, network modernization, and classified mission support. Its portfolio spans from tactical edge technologies and electronic warfare systems to large-scale enterprise IT, digital transformation, and mission support services that underpin critical government operations worldwide.
keyProducts: SkyTracker counter-UAS system
BEAM (Bootable Emergency Access Mechanism) cyber toolkit
Spectral Sieve signals intelligence processing suite
SOF-ISR and multi-INT mission solutions portfolio
Enterprise IT and network modernization services for DoD and IC
notableContracts: U.S. Army Intelligence and Security Command (INSCOM) multi-billion-dollar intelligence and electronic warfare support contracts
Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA) and DoD enterprise IT and network modernization programs
Classified Intelligence Community contracts for multi-INT analysis, cyber operations, and mission support services

name: GE Aerospace
description:
GE Aerospace is a leading global provider of aircraft engines, avionics, and integrated systems for commercial and military aviation. Formerly the aviation division of General Electric, the business was spun off as a standalone company in 2024, focusing on the design, manufacture, and support of propulsion systems and advanced aerospace technologies. The company supplies engines and components for a broad range of platforms, including fighters, bombers, transports, rotorcraft, UAVs, and commercial airliners, and provides digital and maintenance solutions to sustain fleets worldwide.

In the defense sector, GE Aerospace is a key propulsion provider to the U.S. Department of Defense and allied militaries, powering front-line aircraft such as the F‑35, F‑18, and a wide variety of tactical and support platforms. The company also develops next‑generation combat engine technologies under U.S. Air Force and Navy programs, supports upgrade and sustainment efforts for legacy fleets, and delivers avionics and power systems for both manned and unmanned aircraft. With a strong heritage in jet engine innovation dating back to the early days of military aviation, GE Aerospace plays a central role in enabling advanced air combat capabilities and long‑range power projection for U.S. and allied forces.
keyProducts: F110 turbofan engine (F-15, F-16)
F414 turbofan engine (F/A-18E/F, EA-18G)
T700/CT7 turboshaft engine family (UH-60 Black Hawk, AH-64 Apache and variants)
GE Aerospace systems and avionics (power, controls, mission systems)
Next Generation Adaptive Propulsion (NGAP) and XA100 adaptive cycle engine technology
notableContracts: U.S. Navy production and sustainment contracts for F414 engines powering the F/A-18E/F Super Hornet and EA-18G Growler fleets
U.S. Army and foreign military sales contracts for T700 turboshaft engines supporting UH-60 Black Hawk, AH-64 Apache, and allied helicopter fleets
U.S. Air Force research and development contracts for adaptive cycle combat engine technologies under programs such as AETP/NGAP to support future fighter propulsion

name: Honeywell
description:
Honeywell International Inc. is a diversified technology and manufacturing company that plays a significant role in the global defense and aerospace sectors. Through its Honeywell Aerospace and Honeywell Building Technologies businesses, the company provides avionics, engines, environmental control systems, power systems, sensors, navigation, and safety products for military aircraft, helicopters, unmanned systems, and ground vehicles, as well as secure communications and mission systems. Honeywell technologies are integrated into platforms operated by the U.S. Department of Defense, NATO allies, and defense ministries around the world.

Founded in the late 19th and early 20th centuries through predecessor firms, Honeywell has evolved from a controls and heating company into a global industrial and technology leader with a strong defense portfolio. Its systems are found on fighters, transports, rotorcraft, and trainers, and it supplies advanced inertial navigation units, tactical guidance systems, and environmental control systems for both air and space platforms. Honeywell also supports sustainment and modernization programs, providing logistics, repair, and upgrade services that help extend the life and enhance the performance of key military assets.

The company’s defense-related revenues are driven by long-term production, retrofit, and maintenance contracts, often in partnership with prime contractors such as Boeing, Lockheed Martin, and Airbus Defence & Space. Honeywell’s focus on avionics, propulsion, power and thermal management, and high-reliability electronics keeps it at the core of mission-critical systems across the U.S. and allied fleets, while its investments in advanced materials, autonomy, and secure connectivity aim to position it for future defense platforms and next-generation battle management and space architectures.
keyProducts: Honeywell T55 turboshaft engine (for CH-47 Chinook and variants)
Honeywell HTF7000/AS907 engine family for military and special-mission aircraft
Military avionics suites and flight management systems (FMS) for fixed- and rotary-wing platforms
Inertial navigation and guidance systems (including ring laser gyro and MEMS-based INS)
Environmental control and power management systems for military aircraft and space platforms
notableContracts: Long-term support and upgrade contracts for T55 engines powering the U.S. Army’s CH-47 Chinook fleet
Avionics and navigation systems for various U.S. and allied military aircraft, including FMS and cockpit upgrades on transport and tanker platforms
Supply of environmental control, power, and avionics systems for multiple NASA and U.S. Department of Defense space and launch programs through prime contractors

name: KBR
description:
KBR, Inc. is a global engineering, technology, and professional services company that plays a major role in supporting U.S. and allied defense operations worldwide. Historically known for large-scale engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) projects, KBR has evolved into a provider of mission-critical services, including base operations, logistics, systems engineering, cyber and IT, and space and missile defense support. The company is a key contractor for the U.S. Department of Defense and other government agencies, delivering contingency logistics, operational support, and technical services in theater and at installations around the globe.

KBR’s Government Solutions business focuses heavily on defense and national security, supporting the U.S. Army, Navy, Air Force, Space Force, intelligence community, and allied militaries. The company is widely recognized for its work on the Logistics Civil Augmentation Program (LOGCAP), prepositioned stock and readiness support, and for providing engineering and integration services to NASA and the Missile Defense Agency. Over the past decade, KBR has also expanded into cybersecurity, digital engineering, and space systems support, reflecting a strategic shift from traditional construction toward higher-value technical and professional services that underpin modern defense missions.
keyProducts: LOGCAP support services and contingency operations
Army Prepositioned Stocks and global logistics support
NASA engineering, mission support, and human spaceflight services
U.S. and allied missile defense systems engineering and integration support
Cyber, IT, and digital engineering services for defense and intelligence customers
notableContracts: U.S. Army Logistics Civil Augmentation Program (LOGCAP) support task orders
Long-term NASA engineering and mission support contracts across multiple centers
U.S. Missile Defense Agency and DoD systems engineering, integration, and test support programs

name: Advanced Technology International
description:
Advanced Technology International (ATI) is a U.S.-based nonprofit organization that specializes in managing large-scale research and development collaborations between the government, industry, and academia. Best known for its leadership in Other Transaction Authority (OTA) consortia, ATI serves as an integrator and program manager for complex technology programs across defense, homeland security, and national security missions. The organization focuses on accelerating the transition of emerging technologies into operational capabilities by simplifying acquisition processes, broadening the industrial base, and engaging both traditional and non-traditional defense contractors.

Founded in the late 1990s, ATI has become a key player in the U.S. defense innovation ecosystem, administering numerous high-profile consortia in areas such as armaments, shipbuilding, undersea systems, medical technologies, and advanced materials. Its role is primarily as a neutral, trusted intermediary that handles program management, contracting, compliance, and stakeholder coordination, enabling government sponsors to rapidly prototype, test, and field new technologies. With the vast majority of its revenue tied to defense programs, ATI plays a critical behind-the-scenes role in modernizing U.S. military capabilities while expanding opportunities for innovative small and mid-sized firms to participate in defense R&D.
keyProducts: National Armaments Consortium (NAC) / DoD Ordnance Technology Consortium (DOTC) management
Naval Shipbuilding and Advanced Manufacturing (NSAM) consortium management
National Shipbuilding Research Program (NSRP) program management
Medical Technology Enterprise Consortium (MTEC) program administration
Undersea Technology Innovation Consortium (UTIC) management
notableContracts: Management and administration of the DoD Ordnance Technology Consortium (DOTC) OTA for armaments and munitions R&D
Program management support for the Naval Shipbuilding and Advanced Manufacturing (NSAM) and National Shipbuilding Research Program (NSRP) initiatives with the U.S. Navy
Administration of the Medical Technology Enterprise Consortium (MTEC) OTA supporting U.S. Army Medical Research and Development Command (USAMRDC) medical R&D programs

name: Peraton
description:
Peraton is a leading U.S. national security and technology firm that provides mission-critical services and solutions to defense, intelligence, space, cyber, and federal civilian customers. Formed through the divestiture of Harris Corporation’s government IT services business and subsequently expanded through acquisitions such as Northrop Grumman’s federal IT and mission support business and Perspecta, Peraton focuses on complex, high-consequence missions including cyber defense, space domain awareness, C5ISR, and intelligence analysis. The company supports customers across the Department of Defense, Intelligence Community, Department of Homeland Security, NASA, and other federal agencies.

Peraton plays a significant role in U.S. defense and national security by delivering advanced cyber operations, secure communications, data analytics, and large-scale mission support. Its portfolio spans enterprise IT and cloud modernization, space and satellite ground systems operations, signals and geospatial intelligence processing, electronic warfare support, and critical infrastructure protection. With deep expertise in operating and sustaining highly secure, classified environments, Peraton is a key integrator and long-term partner for government programs that require resiliency, technological sophistication, and 24/7 operational readiness.
keyProducts: Cyber and Defensive Cyber Operations (DCO) services for DoD and Intelligence Community customers
Mission IT and enterprise cloud modernization solutions for federal agencies
Space and satellite ground systems operations and space domain awareness support
C5ISR and mission support services for defense and intelligence programs
Data analytics, SIGINT/GEOINT processing, and mission analytics platforms
notableContracts: NASA Space Communications and Navigation (SCaN) and space/ground network support contracts
U.S. Department of Homeland Security and DOJ cyber and enterprise IT support task orders under major IDIQ vehicles
U.S. Department of Defense and Intelligence Community mission support and C5ISR contracts, including large multi-year task orders inherited through the Perspecta acquisition

name: Parsons Corporation
description:
Parsons Corporation is a U.S.-based technology-focused defense and infrastructure contractor specializing in cybersecurity, intelligence, space, missile defense, and critical infrastructure solutions. The company provides systems engineering, digital engineering, C4ISR integration, cyber operations, and mission support services to the U.S. Department of Defense, Intelligence Community, Department of Homeland Security, and allied governments. Parsons has increasingly positioned itself as a high-end, software- and data-driven defense firm, emphasizing rapid integration of AI/ML, digital twins, and autonomous systems into complex mission environments.

Founded as an engineering and construction firm, Parsons has evolved through organic growth and acquisitions into a major player in defense, space, and national security markets, while retaining a strong presence in civil infrastructure and transportation. The company plays a key role in missile defense command-and-control, space domain awareness, cyber and electronic warfare, and critical infrastructure protection, often serving as a prime integrator and mission partner on long-duration, technically demanding programs. Its work supports strategic deterrence, integrated air and missile defense, and the protection of military and civilian networks and facilities worldwide.
keyProducts: Parsons Defense & Intelligence cyber operations and mission support solutions
Space and missile defense command-and-control and integration services (including IAMD/BMD support)
C4ISR and multi-domain operations integration and digital engineering platforms
Space domain awareness, satellite command-and-control, and ground systems support
Critical infrastructure protection and secure smart base/installation modernization solutions
notableContracts: U.S. Air Force and Missile Defense Agency engineering and integration support for integrated air and missile defense and missile warning/defense architectures
U.S. Space Force and other national security space customers – space domain awareness, ground systems, and mission support contracts

U.S. Department of Defense and Intelligence Community cyber operations, C4ISR, and digital engineering contracts supporting multi-domain and joint all-domain command and control (JADC2)-aligned efforts

name: SpaceX
description:
SpaceX (Space Exploration Technologies Corp.) is a private aerospace and space-transportation company that designs, manufactures, and launches advanced rockets and spacecraft. Founded by Elon Musk, the company’s mission is to reduce the cost of access to space and enable the colonization of Mars. SpaceX develops its launch vehicles and spacecraft largely in-house, including the Falcon family of rockets, the Dragon spacecraft, and the next-generation Starship system. The company also operates the Starlink constellation, a global broadband network built on thousands of low Earth orbit (LEO) satellites.

In the defense and national security arena, SpaceX has become a major launch provider and space infrastructure partner for the U.S. government. It conducts national security space launches for the U.S. Space Force and intelligence community, provides secure satellite communications via Starlink for defense and allied customers, and supports rapid, responsive launch capabilities. SpaceX’s reusable launch technology and growing Starlink constellation have significantly reshaped the military space and communications landscape, offering lower-cost, high-tempo launch options and resilient, distributed satellite networks.

SpaceX continues to expand its role in defense through classified and unclassified missions, participation in National Security Space Launch (NSSL) programs, and experimental efforts such as point-to-point cargo delivery concepts for the Department of Defense. Its combination of high launch cadence, reusable rockets, and scalable satellite manufacturing positions it as a key strategic partner for U.S. and allied space and defense initiatives.
keyProducts: Falcon 9 launch vehicle
Falcon Heavy launch vehicle
Dragon spacecraft (Cargo Dragon & Crew Dragon)
Starship launch system
Starlink satellite communications constellation
notableContracts: National Security Space Launch (NSSL) Phase 2 launch services for the U.S. Space Force
Commercial Crew Program contracts with NASA for Crew Dragon missions to the International Space Station
U.S. Space Force and Department of Defense Starlink and secure SATCOM support contracts

name: V2X, Inc.
description:
V2X, Inc. is a U.S.-based defense and government services contractor specializing in mission support, base operations, training, and technical services for the U.S. Department of Defense and allied governments. Formed through the 2022 merger of Vectrus and Vertex, V2X combines long-standing expertise in logistics, aviation services, and infrastructure with modern digital and cybersecurity capabilities. The company supports operations on military installations, in deployed environments, and at critical facilities worldwide, providing life-cycle sustainment, readiness support, and complex technical services.

V2X plays a significant role in enabling global military readiness by delivering integrated logistics, operations & maintenance, and training solutions across land, air, and maritime environments. Its portfolio spans base operations support, prepositioned equipment management, aviation and aerospace maintenance, C5ISR support, and IT and cyber services. With a large deployed workforce and extensive experience in contingency and expeditionary environments, V2X is a key partner to the U.S. Army, Air Force, Navy, Marine Corps, and various defense agencies, helping ensure mission readiness and continuity in demanding and austere locations.
keyProducts: Global base operations & installation support services
Army and Air Force logistics and prepositioned equipment management programs
Aviation and aerospace maintenance, repair, and overhaul (MRO) services
C5ISR field support and technical services
Training, simulation, and readiness support programs
notableContracts: U.S. Army Logistics Civil Augmentation Program (LOGCAP) and related theater support contracts
U.S. Air Force base operations and installation support services at CONUS and OCONUS locations
Aviation and aerospace MRO and support contracts for U.S. military rotary- and fixed-wing fleets

name: Science Applications International Corporation (SAIC)
description:
Science Applications International Corporation (SAIC) is a major U.S. government services and technology integrator that provides engineering, IT modernization, cybersecurity, and mission support primarily to the Department of Defense, intelligence community, and federal civil agencies. SAIC specializes in systems integration, digital engineering, cloud migration, and managed services, helping customers modernize legacy systems, secure critical networks, and operate complex, mission-critical environments.

Founded in 1969, SAIC has evolved from a scientific and engineering consulting firm into a large publicly traded defense contractor and IT solutions provider. The company plays a key role in U.S. defense and national security by supporting command-and-control systems, enterprise IT environments, space and ISR ground systems, and logistics and training solutions across all military services. Through a combination of organic growth and acquisitions, including the integration of Engility and Unisys Federal, SAIC has expanded its portfolio to include large-scale enterprise IT transformation, cloud and zero-trust cybersecurity initiatives, and advanced analytics and AI services for both defense and federal civilian customers.
keyProducts: Enterprise IT modernization and managed services for DoD and federal agencies
Cloud migration and zero-trust cybersecurity solutions for classified and unclassified networks
Space and ISR ground systems engineering and integration support
Model-based systems engineering (MBSE) and digital engineering services for defense platforms
Training, simulation, and mission support solutions for U.S. Army and other services
notableContracts: U.S. Army Enterprise IT As-a-Service and network modernization contracts
Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA) and DoD enterprise IT and cybersecurity support contracts
NASA and U.S. Space Force space and ground-systems engineering and integration support programs

name: Textron Inc.
description:
Textron Inc. is a diversified aerospace, defense, and industrial conglomerate best known in the defense sector through its Bell, Textron Systems, and Textron Aviation brands. The company designs and manufactures military and commercial helicopters, tiltrotor aircraft, unmanned systems, precision weapons, armored vehicles, and advanced training and mission support solutions. Its portfolio spans rotary-wing platforms such as the V-22 Osprey (in partnership with Boeing) and Bell V-280 Valor, light attack and trainer aircraft like the Beechcraft T-6 Texan II and AT-6 Wolverine, as well as unmanned surface and aerial systems, marine craft, and weaponized sensor platforms.

Founded in 1923 as a textile company, Textron evolved over the decades through acquisitions and diversification into a major aerospace and defense player. Today it plays a central role in U.S. and allied defense modernization programs, particularly in vertical lift, Future Vertical Lift (FVL) initiatives, and tactical aviation and ground systems. The company supports U.S. Army, Air Force, Navy, Marine Corps, and international customers with production, modernization, sustainment, and training services, contributing both advanced platforms and enabling technologies across the battlespace.
keyProducts: Bell V-22 Osprey (with Boeing)
Bell V-280 Valor
Bell AH-1Z Viper and UH-1Y Venom
Beechcraft T-6 Texan II and AT-6 Wolverine
Textron Systems unmanned surface and aerial systems (e.g., Common Unmanned Surface Vehicle)
notableContracts: U.S. Army Future Long Range Assault Aircraft (FLRAA) program – Bell V-280 Valor selection
U.S. Department of Defense production and sustainment contracts for V-22 Osprey tiltrotor aircraft (with Boeing)
U.S. and international T-6 Texan II trainer aircraft procurement and support contracts

name: TransDigm
description:
TransDigm Group Incorporated is a major designer, producer, and supplier of highly engineered aircraft components used on nearly all commercial and military aircraft platforms. Operating primarily through a decentralized model of specialized business units, the company focuses on proprietary, niche products that are critical to aircraft performance and safety, such as actuators, ignition systems, pumps and valves, latching and locking devices, cockpit security systems, and various electro-mechanical components. Its parts are typically characterized by high engineering content, stringent certification requirements, and long lifecycles, allowing TransDigm to maintain strong positions across OEM production and aftermarket support.

Founded in 1993, TransDigm has grown largely through acquisitions of aerospace component manufacturers with strong aftermarket exposure, building a portfolio that spans flight safety, power and control systems, and airframe-related products. In the defense sector, its components are installed on a wide array of U.S. and allied military aircraft, including fighters, transports, rotorcraft, and special-mission platforms, as well as some naval aviation and space-related systems. By providing mission‑critical hardware with rigorous reliability and performance requirements, TransDigm plays an important role in readiness, sustainment, and modernization for both U.S. Department of Defense customers and international defense ministries.
keyProducts: Aircraft actuators and control systems (primary and secondary flight control actuators, seat and cargo actuators)
Ignition systems and engine-related components for military and commercial aircraft
Aircraft pumps, valves, and power & control components for fuel, hydraulic, and pneumatic systems
Cockpit and cabin security, latching, and locking mechanisms (including reinforced cockpit security systems)
Critical avionics and aircraft electrical components (sensors, switches, and specialized connectors)
notableContracts: Long-term supply and sustainment agreements for mission‑critical components on U.S. Air Force and U.S. Navy fixed‑wing and rotary‑wing fleets
OEM production and aftermarket support contracts for proprietary components on major platforms such as the F-35, F/A-18, C-17, and various UH/CH-series helicopters
Multi-year spares, repairs, and overhaul support agreements with the Defense Logistics Agency (DLA) and international air forces for TransDigm portfolio components

name: Bechtel National, Inc.
description:
Bechtel National, Inc. is the U.S. government services and nuclear security arm of Bechtel, one of the world’s largest engineering, construction, and project management companies. Bechtel National focuses on large, complex projects for the U.S. Department of Defense, Department of Energy, and other federal agencies, particularly in nuclear facilities, national security infrastructure, and environmental cleanup. Its work spans the design, construction, and operation of high-hazard facilities, including nuclear weapons infrastructure, chemical weapons destruction plants, and waste treatment facilities at some of the most challenging legacy sites in the U.S. defense complex.

The company plays a critical role in sustaining U.S. national security and treaty compliance by delivering secure, resilient, and highly regulated facilities and programs. Bechtel National has been a prime or lead contractor on multiple multi-decade projects at DOE and DoD installations, integrating advanced engineering, safety, and project management disciplines. Its contributions include modernizing aging nuclear infrastructure, safely treating and disposing of hazardous and radioactive waste from Cold War–era weapons production, and supporting complex test ranges and research facilities that underpin U.S. defense capabilities.
keyProducts: Hanford Waste Treatment and Immobilization Plant (WTP) – engineering, construction, and commissioning
Pueblo and Blue Grass Chemical Agent-Destruction Pilot Plants – design, build, and operations support
NNSA nuclear security and weapons infrastructure modernization programs (various sites)
Environmental management and legacy waste remediation programs at DOE defense sites
Large-scale government program management and EPC services for classified and high-hazard facilities
notableContracts: U.S. Department of Energy contract for the Hanford Tank Waste Treatment and Immobilization Plant, Richland, Washington
Prime/lead roles on chemical weapons destruction plants at Pueblo, Colorado and Blue Grass, Kentucky for the U.S. Army and DoD
Multiple long-term National Nuclear Security Administration (NNSA) and DOE Environmental Management contracts for nuclear security infrastructure and cleanup projects

name: Oshkosh
description:
Oshkosh Corporation is a U.S.-based manufacturer specializing in purpose-built vehicles and equipment for defense, emergency services, and commercial markets. Through its Oshkosh Defense business, the company designs and produces tactical wheeled vehicles for the U.S. Department of Defense and allied militaries, including trucks for transport, logistics, and protected mobility. Its platforms focus on survivability, off-road performance, and lifecycle support, making them core elements of ground force mobility and sustainment.

Founded in 1917, Oshkosh has evolved from a regional truck manufacturer into a diversified industrial and defense contractor with a global footprint. In the defense sector, the company is best known for its role as a major supplier of tactical vehicles to the U.S. Army and Marine Corps, including the Joint Light Tactical Vehicle (JLTV) family and a wide range of medium and heavy tactical trucks. Oshkosh pairs vehicle design and manufacturing with training, field service, and sustainment support, helping military customers maintain readiness over long service lives and in demanding operational environments.
keyProducts: Joint Light Tactical Vehicle (JLTV)
Family of Medium Tactical Vehicles (FMTV A2)
Heavy Expanded Mobility Tactical Truck (HEMTT)
Palettized Load System (PLS)
Oshkosh Defense armored and protected mobility vehicle family
notableContracts: U.S. Army and U.S. Marine Corps Joint Light Tactical Vehicle (JLTV) production and support contracts
U.S. Army Family of Medium Tactical Vehicles (FMTV A2) production contract
Multiple U.S. Department of Defense contracts for recapitalization, field service, and lifecycle support of heavy and medium tactical wheeled vehicles

name: BWX Technologies, Inc.
description:
BWX Technologies, Inc. (BWXT) is a U.S.-based nuclear technology and manufacturing company that plays a critical role in national defense, particularly in naval nuclear propulsion and advanced nuclear systems. The company designs and manufactures naval nuclear reactors, reactor components, and fuel for the U.S. Navy’s nuclear-powered aircraft carriers and submarines, and supports a range of nuclear operations, environmental management, and technical services for U.S. government agencies. BWXT is a key industrial partner to the Navy and the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE), providing highly specialized capabilities that few other companies can match.

With roots tracing back to the early 20th century through predecessors like Babcock & Wilcox, BWX Technologies was established as a separate publicly traded company in 2015 after a corporate reorganization. Today, BWXT is a major supplier within the U.S. nuclear enterprise, supporting defense, national security, and space-related nuclear power initiatives. The company’s work includes manufacturing nuclear components at secure facilities, managing and operating government-owned nuclear sites, and developing advanced microreactors and nuclear thermal propulsion concepts for future defense and space applications.
keyProducts: U.S. Navy nuclear propulsion components and reactors for aircraft carriers
U.S. Navy nuclear propulsion components and reactors for submarines
Nuclear fuel fabrication for naval and government programs
Advanced nuclear microreactor and small modular reactor (SMR) concepts
Nuclear thermal propulsion and space nuclear power systems (R&D)
notableContracts: Long-term U.S. Navy contracts for nuclear propulsion components and fuel for Virginia-class submarines and Ford-class aircraft carriers
U.S. Department of Energy and National Nuclear Security Administration (NNSA) contracts for nuclear facility management, environmental remediation, and component manufacturing
Development and demonstration contracts with NASA and DOE for advanced space nuclear power and nuclear thermal propulsion technologies

name: Viasat
description:
Viasat is a U.S.-based global communications company specializing in high-capacity satellite networks, secure defense communications, and broadband services for government, military, and commercial customers. The company designs and operates satellite constellations, develops ground and airborne communication terminals, and provides managed network services that support everything from in-flight connectivity and maritime broadband to beyond-line-of-sight (BLOS) tactical communications for deployed forces. Through its acquisition of Inmarsat, Viasat has significantly expanded its global satellite fleet and spectrum rights, positioning itself as a major player in multi-orbit, global broadband and mobility services.

In the defense sector, Viasat is a key provider of tactical data links, satellite communications (SATCOM), and secure networking solutions for the U.S. Department of Defense and allied militaries. Its technologies enable resilient, encrypted connectivity for command and control, ISR platforms, ground vehicles, and dismounted troops. Viasat supports programs such as Link 16 tactical data links, protected satellite waveforms, and modernized battlefield networks that underpin joint all-domain operations. The company plays a prominent role in advancing resilient, secure, and interoperable communications across air, land, sea, and space domains.
keyProducts: Viasat Link 16 Tactical Data Link Terminals (including MIDS and small form-factor radios)
Viasat SATCOM Solutions for Government & Defense (fixed, mobile, and airborne terminals)
Viasat High-Capacity Ka-band Satellite Network (including ViaSat-2 and ViaSat-3-class systems)
Viasat Information Assurance & Encryption Products (Type 1 and high-grade crypto solutions)
Airborne In-Flight Connectivity and Mobility SATCOM Services for government and commercial fleets
notableContracts: U.S. Department of Defense contracts for Link 16 tactical data link terminals and modernized tactical networking radios across multiple services
U.S. government and allied nation SATCOM contracts providing beyond-line-of-sight, secure broadband services for airborne, maritime, and ground platforms
Multi-year contracts with U.S. and international defense ministries for secure satellite communications, encryption, and network management services leveraging Viasat’s global satellite fleet

name: Sierra Nevada Corp.
description:
Sierra Nevada Corporation (SNC) is a privately held U.S. aerospace and defense company known for its work in space systems, ISR (intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance), electronic warfare, and mission-critical aviation services. The company develops and integrates advanced systems for the U.S. Department of Defense, NASA, intelligence agencies, and allied governments, with capabilities spanning spacecraft, mission systems, cyber and EW solutions, and specialized aircraft modifications. SNC is particularly recognized for its Dream Chaser spaceplane, tactical mission systems, and rapidly deployable communication and sensor platforms.

Founded as a small high-technology firm, SNC has grown through both organic innovation and targeted acquisitions into a mid-tier prime contractor with a strong presence across space, airborne, and ground-based mission domains. The company plays a significant role in national security space programs, special operations support, and battlefield communications, often focusing on agile, rapidly fielded solutions. Its portfolio aligns closely with U.S. modernization priorities, including responsive space, contested EW environments, and resilient, networked C4ISR architectures.
keyProducts: Dream Chaser spaceplane and cargo system
SNC Space Systems satellite buses and components
Tactical Mission Systems for ISR and special operations aircraft
Electronic warfare and signals intelligence (SIGINT) payloads
Integrated communication, networking, and mission management systems
notableContracts: NASA Commercial Resupply Services 2 (CRS-2) contract using Dream Chaser to service the ISS
U.S. Department of Defense and Special Operations Command contracts for modified ISR and missionized aircraft
U.S. national security space and payload integration contracts supporting classified and unclassified satellite programs

name: Parker Hannifin Corporation
description:
Parker Hannifin Corporation is a global leader in motion and control technologies, supplying critical components and systems for aerospace, defense, industrial, and mobile applications. In the defense sector, Parker provides hydraulic, pneumatic, electromechanical, and filtration solutions that are integrated into aircraft, missiles, ground vehicles, and naval platforms. Its products support flight control, fuel and engine systems, landing gear, braking, and environmental control, making Parker a key subsystem provider to major prime contractors and defense OEMs worldwide.

Founded in 1917, Parker has grown from a small motion-control firm into a diversified technology company with a strong presence in both commercial and military aerospace. The company’s aerospace segment, bolstered by acquisitions such as Meggitt, plays a significant role in U.S. and allied defense programs, including fighters, transports, rotorcraft, and missiles. While defense represents a portion of its overall revenue, Parker’s components and systems are often mission-critical, supporting reliability, safety, and performance across a broad range of U.S. Department of Defense and international defense platforms.
keyProducts: Aerospace hydraulic and fuel systems for military aircraft (flight controls, pumps, valves, actuators)
Environmental control and engine bleed air systems for fighter and transport aircraft
Aircraft braking and landing gear systems (including Meggitt braking solutions)
Fuel and inerting systems for fixed-wing, rotorcraft, and missile platforms
Filtration and fluid management systems for aerospace and defense applications
notableContracts: Long-term supply agreements for hydraulic, fuel, and flight control components on the F-35 Lightning II program
OEM and aftermarket support contracts for hydraulic, fuel, and braking systems on U.S. and allied military transport and tanker fleets (e.g., C-130/J, KC-46)
Multi-year sustainment and MRO agreements for defense aerospace components with the U.S. Department of Defense and major primes such as Lockheed Martin, Boeing, and Airbus Defence & Space

name: Curtiss-Wright Corporation
description:
Curtiss-Wright Corporation is a diversified engineering company that provides highly engineered products, systems, and services to the defense, aerospace, and industrial markets. The company’s defense portfolio includes advanced actuation and motion control systems, embedded computing and electronics, avionics, weapons handling systems, naval propulsion components, and nuclear instrumentation and control systems. Curtiss-Wright supports a broad range of U.S. and allied military platforms, including tactical aircraft, helicopters, ground vehicles, missiles, submarines, aircraft carriers, and nuclear-powered naval vessels.

The company traces its roots to the early days of aviation, formed in 1929 through the merger of companies associated with aviation pioneers Glenn Curtiss and the Wright brothers. Over the decades, Curtiss-Wright evolved from an aircraft manufacturer into a technology and engineering firm focused on critical defense and industrial applications. Today, it plays a key role in mission-critical systems for programs such as advanced fighter jets, the U.S. Navy’s nuclear fleet, and strategic missile and radar systems, leveraging its expertise in motion control, power electronics, ruggedized computing, and safety-critical technologies.
keyProducts: Motion and flight control actuation systems for military aircraft and missiles
Rugged embedded computing and COTS electronics (e.g., defense-grade VPX and OpenVPX systems)
Shipboard and submarine propulsion components and pumps for U.S. Navy nuclear vessels
Nuclear instrumentation and control systems for naval reactors
Aircraft arresting and landing systems for military airfields and carriers
notableContracts: U.S. Navy contracts to supply pumps, valves, and critical components for nuclear-powered aircraft carriers and submarines, including the Gerald R. Ford–class carriers and Virginia-class submarines
Long-term agreements to provide embedded computing, data acquisition, and processing systems for U.S. and allied tactical aircraft, ISR platforms, and missile defense systems
Contracts with the U.S. Department of Defense and prime contractors for flight control actuation and weapons handling systems on various fixed-wing, rotary-wing, and missile platforms

name: Amphenol
description:
Amphenol Corporation is one of the world’s largest manufacturers of interconnect products, specializing in connectors, cable assemblies, antennas, and high‑speed/high‑reliability interconnect systems. Across commercial, industrial, automotive, and communications markets, Amphenol provides mission‑critical connectivity solutions that operate in harsh environments and under demanding performance requirements. In the defense sector, the company supplies ruggedized electrical, optical, RF/microwave, and fiber‑optic interconnect systems used in avionics, ground vehicles, naval platforms, missiles, radars, and communications networks.

Founded in 1932, Amphenol has grown into a major global supplier to leading defense primes and government agencies, providing highly engineered products that support C4ISR, electronic warfare, sensors, and platform mission systems. Its connectors and cable assemblies are designed for extreme reliability in battlefield conditions—resisting shock, vibration, temperature, and electromagnetic interference—making them integral to modern aerospace and defense architectures. As defense systems become more networked and data‑intensive, Amphenol’s high‑speed and high‑density interconnect technologies play a key role in enabling advanced electronics, secure communications, and space‑qualified payloads for U.S. and allied militaries.
keyProducts: Mil‑DTL‑38999 and other circular military/aerospace connectors
Ruggedized RF and microwave coaxial connectors and cable assemblies for EW and radar
High‑speed data and fiber‑optic interconnect systems for avionics and mission computers
Harsh‑environment Ethernet and networking interconnect solutions for ground and naval platforms
Space‑qualified connectors and harnesses for satellites and launch vehicles
notableContracts: Multi‑year supply agreements to provide MIL‑qualified circular connectors and harnesses for U.S. and allied military aircraft and rotorcraft fleets
Long‑term interconnect supply programs supporting major U.S. ground combat vehicle and tactical communications system upgrades
Space‑rated connector and cable assembly contracts for U.S. and European government and commercial satellite constellations and launch providers

name: Palantir Technologies
description:
Palantir Technologies is a U.S.-based software company specializing in data integration, analytics, and decision-support platforms for government, defense, and commercial customers. The company’s core products—Palantir Gotham, Palantir Foundry, and Palantir Apollo—enable users to fuse large volumes of disparate data, run complex analytics and AI/ML models, and collaborate on operational decisions in real time. In the defense domain, Palantir’s platforms are used for intelligence analysis, targeting, mission planning, logistics optimization, and battle management, often operating at multiple classification levels and in austere, edge-compute environments.

Founded in 2003 by Peter Thiel, Alex Karp, and a group of PayPal alumni, Palantir initially focused on counterterrorism and intelligence applications after 9/11, working closely with elements of the U.S. intelligence community and law enforcement. Over time, it expanded into broader defense missions and allied government markets, while also building a significant commercial business in sectors such as manufacturing, energy, and healthcare. In defense, Palantir has become a key provider of open-architecture, AI-enabled software for the U.S. Department of Defense and NATO partners, supporting programs ranging from space domain awareness to joint all-domain command and control (JADC2) and mission command at the tactical edge.

Today, Palantir plays a prominent role in modernizing defense and national security data infrastructures, emphasizing rapid software deployment, modular open systems, and human-centric AI tools for analysts, operators, and commanders. Its platforms underpin operational decision-making for U.S. and allied forces, supporting use cases such as integrated targeting, kill chain automation, logistics and sustainment analytics, and real-time situational awareness in active theaters of operation.
keyProducts: Palantir Gotham
Palantir Foundry
Palantir Apollo
Palantir Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP)
MetaConstellation (space-based data orchestration)
notableContracts: U.S. Army Vantage program and follow-on analytics platforms for enterprise data integration and decision support
U.S. Special Operations Command (USSOCOM) and U.S. Air Force contracts for AI/ML-enabled mission command and JADC2-related capabilities
U.S. Space Force and U.S. Space Command contracts supporting space domain awareness and space operations analytics using MetaConstellation and related tools

name: MITRE
description:
MITRE is a not-for-profit organization that operates multiple Federally Funded Research and Development Centers (FFRDCs) for U.S. government agencies, with a central role in systems engineering, applied research, and technology integration for national security. It supports the Department of Defense, the intelligence community, the Federal Aviation Administration, the Department of Homeland Security, and numerous civil agencies by providing independent technical expertise, objective analysis, and long-term R&D. MITRE is best known for helping government sponsors architect complex enterprise systems, from command-and-control networks and cyber defense architectures to air traffic management and secure communications.

Founded during the Cold War to support advanced air defense and command-and-control systems, MITRE has evolved into a broad-based national security and public-interest R&D organization. It is structured as an independent, not-for-profit corporation without commercial products, allowing it to act as an impartial adviser and systems engineer across military, intelligence, and civilian missions. MITRE’s work spans emerging technologies such as AI/ML, cyber operations, advanced networking, space and ISR architectures, and data analytics, often shaping standards, reference architectures, and prototypes that are later transitioned to government programs of record and the defense industrial base.
keyProducts: MITRE ATT&CK framework
Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) stewardship and related cyber programs
Next-generation command, control, communications, computers, and intelligence (C4I) architectures for DoD and IC sponsors
Air traffic management and NextGen/NAS systems engineering support for FAA
Enterprise analytics, AI/ML, and decision-support prototypes for national security agencies
notableContracts: Operation of the National Security Engineering Center (NSEC) FFRDC for the Department of Defense and the intelligence community
Operation of the Homeland Security Systems Engineering and Development Institute (HSSEDI) FFRDC for the Department of Homeland Security
Operation of the Center for Advanced Aviation System Development (CAASD) FFRDC for the Federal Aviation Administration and related national airspace modernization efforts

name: Moog Inc.
description:
Moog Inc. is a U.S.-based designer and manufacturer of precision motion control products and systems used in aerospace, defense, industrial, and space applications. The company’s technologies enable the precise control of flight surfaces, missiles, launch vehicles, satellites, and advanced weapons systems, as well as high‑performance industrial machinery. Moog’s portfolio spans electromechanical, electrohydraulic, and electronic control solutions, including actuators, servo valves, flight control computers, and integrated control systems.

Founded in 1951, Moog has grown from a small servo valve producer into a global motion-control specialist with a significant role in both commercial and military aerospace markets. Its products are embedded in numerous U.S. and allied aircraft, missile systems, and space platforms, where reliability and precision are mission critical. In defense, Moog supports combat aircraft, rotorcraft, guided weapons, naval platforms, and space systems, often as a key subsystem provider to major prime contractors. The company is known for its engineering depth, customization capabilities, and long-term support of legacy and next-generation defense programs.
keyProducts: Flight control actuation systems for military and commercial aircraft
Electrohydraulic and electromechanical servovalves and actuators
Missile and guided weapon steering and fin actuation systems
Space vehicle and launch vehicle motion control systems
Naval and turret motion-control and stabilization systems
notableContracts: Flight control actuation and motion control systems for multiple U.S. and allied fighter and transport aircraft platforms (including F‑35 and other tactical aircraft subsystems via major primes)
Actuation and motion-control solutions for U.S. missile and precision-guided munition programs with the Department of Defense and major missile OEMs
Space and launch vehicle motion-control and actuation contracts supporting NASA and commercial launch providers for rockets, spacecraft, and payload platforms

name: ManTech
description:
ManTech is a U.S.-based defense and intelligence services contractor specializing in cyber, IT modernization, mission systems engineering, and advanced analytics for federal agencies. The company provides technology and engineering solutions to the Department of Defense, Intelligence Community, Department of Homeland Security, and a range of civilian agencies, with a strong focus on secure enterprise IT, cyber defense, cloud migration, and C4ISR support. ManTech is known for operating in highly classified environments and supporting national security missions across the full lifecycle—from R&D and prototyping through operations, sustainment, and modernization.

Founded during the rise of modern systems engineering in the late 1960s, ManTech has evolved from a small technical services provider into a major mid-tier national security contractor. The firm plays a key role in protecting federal networks, enabling secure communications for deployed forces, and providing advanced data analytics and engineering support for intelligence and space-related programs. By combining cyber operations, digital transformation, and mission engineering expertise, ManTech supports some of the U.S. government’s most sensitive and demanding defense and intelligence programs worldwide.
keyProducts: Cyber defense and hunt support services for DoD and Intelligence Community networks
Enterprise IT modernization and cloud migration services for federal agencies
C4ISR engineering and mission systems integration for defense and intel customers
Secure software development, DevSecOps, and digital engineering toolchains
Data analytics, AI/ML, and mission analytics solutions for national security missions
notableContracts: U.S. Department of Defense cyber and enterprise IT support contracts, including support to key Combatant Commands
Intelligence Community mission IT, systems engineering, and analytic support task orders under multiple classified IDIQ vehicles
Department of Homeland Security cybersecurity and systems engineering support contracts for critical national security programs

name: HEICO
description:
HEICO Corporation is a U.S.-based aerospace and electronics manufacturer and service provider specializing in jet engine and aircraft component replacement parts, aerospace electronics, and mission‑critical subsystems. Through its Flight Support Group and Electronic Technologies Group, HEICO supplies FAA-approved aftermarket parts (PMAs), repair and overhaul services, and high‑reliability electronic components used in military aircraft, missiles, helicopters, unmanned systems, and space platforms. Its products are widely used by the U.S. Department of Defense, prime contractors, and international defense ministries for sustainment, modernization, and cost‑reduction initiatives.

Founded in 1957, HEICO has grown from a small aviation components firm into a diversified, acquisition‑driven company with dozens of operating subsidiaries serving both commercial and defense markets. In defense, HEICO’s portfolio includes power conversion equipment, RF and microwave components, infrared simulation and test gear, high‑rel components for space and missiles, and specialty interconnect and sensor products. By focusing on niche, highly engineered components and sustainment solutions, HEICO plays a key role in keeping legacy and in‑service aircraft, missile systems, and space assets operational at lower lifecycle cost for U.S. and allied forces.
keyProducts: FAA-approved PMA replacement parts and components for military and commercial jet engines and airframes
High-reliability RF, microwave, and millimeter-wave components for missile and EW systems
Power conversion and power management modules for aerospace and defense applications
Infrared (IR) simulation and test equipment for missile warning and seeker system validation
Specialty interconnects, high-voltage connectors, and harsh-environment cabling for aircraft, naval, and space platforms
notableContracts: Long-term supply and sustainment agreements to provide PMA replacement parts for U.S. military transport and tanker aircraft fleets
Component supply contracts with major primes (e.g., Lockheed Martin, Boeing, Raytheon, Northrop Grumman) for RF, power, and interconnect hardware used in missile, radar, and EW programs
Contracts to provide high-reliability electronic components and subsystems for classified and unclassified U.S. government and commercial satellite and launch vehicle programs

name: Howmet Aerospace
description:
Howmet Aerospace is a leading global manufacturer of advanced engineered materials and components for the aerospace and defense industries, with a particular focus on high‑performance alloys, structural components, and fastening systems. The company’s portfolio spans aerospace engine and airframe components, forged wheels, and engineered fasteners used across commercial and military aviation, as well as in space, defense, and industrial applications. Its products are critical in high‑temperature, high‑stress environments, such as jet engines and gas turbines, where reliability and performance are paramount.

Howmet Aerospace was formed in 2020 following the separation of Arconic Inc. into two independent companies, but its heritage traces back more than a century through predecessors including Alcoa and Howmet Corporation. Today, Howmet supplies major defense OEMs with turbine blades and vanes, structural castings, and fastening systems used on platforms such as fighter jets, military transports, rotorcraft, and missile systems. The company plays an important role in strengthening U.S. and allied defense industrial bases by providing mission‑critical components that enhance engine efficiency, reduce weight, and improve durability in demanding military operations.
keyProducts: Turbine engine airfoils (blades and vanes) for military and commercial aircraft engines
Aerospace structural castings for airframes and nacelles
Engineered fasteners and fastening systems for defense and aerospace platforms
Aerospace forgings and rings for gas turbine engines and defense applications
Advanced engineered materials and high‑temperature superalloy components
notableContracts: Long-term supply agreements with major engine OEMs (e.g., Pratt & Whitney, GE Aerospace, Rolls‑Royce) for turbine airfoils and structural components used on U.S. and allied military aircraft
Multi‑year contracts to supply aerospace fastening systems and structural hardware for U.S. fighter, tanker, and transport aircraft production programs
Supply arrangements for high‑temperature alloy components and castings supporting U.S. and NATO missile and propulsion systems

name: The Aerospace Corporation
description:
The Aerospace Corporation is a federally funded research and development center (FFRDC) that provides independent technical expertise to the U.S. government on space and national security space systems. Established as a nonprofit, Aerospace supports the U.S. Space Force, U.S. Air Force, the National Reconnaissance Office (NRO), NASA, and other civil and national security space customers across the entire lifecycle of space programs—from early concept development and architecture studies to launch, on-orbit operations, and decommissioning.

The company is known for its deep bench of scientists and engineers specializing in satellite systems, launch vehicles, space domain awareness, mission assurance, and advanced space technologies. Aerospace plays a central role in ensuring mission success and risk reduction for critical national security space assets, providing independent systems engineering, test and evaluation, and readiness assessments for major space programs. Its work increasingly spans resilient space architectures, space traffic management, cybersecurity for space systems, and advanced research in emerging space capabilities.
keyProducts: National Security Space Launch (NSSL) mission assurance support
GPS and next-generation PNT (Positioning, Navigation, and Timing) system engineering support
Space domain awareness and space traffic management tools and analysis
Architectures and systems engineering for missile warning and missile defense space assets
Concept development and systems engineering support for National Reconnaissance Office satellite programs
notableContracts: FFRDC support to U.S. Space Force and U.S. Air Force for National Security Space Launch (NSSL) and legacy EELV mission assurance
Longstanding FFRDC support contract for National Reconnaissance Office space systems engineering and mission assurance
Enterprise systems engineering and architecture support for U.S. Space Force space superiority, missile warning, and resilient space architectures

name: Keysight Technologies
description:
Keysight Technologies is a leading provider of electronic design and test solutions, serving commercial, communications, and aerospace and defense customers worldwide. Spun off from Agilent Technologies in 2014, with roots that trace back to Hewlett-Packard’s test and measurement business, Keysight supplies electronic measurement instruments, simulation software, and test systems that enable the development, verification, and sustainment of advanced RF, microwave, and high‑speed digital systems.
